ontology.foodex2_description: Pasta, whole wheat, dry — mapped under FoodEx2 pasta/noodle category for whole grain dried pasta
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a single dedicated code for wholemeal pasta uncooked; A000F (pasta) is the closest parent code in the FoodEx2 hierarchy for dried whole grain pasta. Whole grain facet descriptor applied to narrow the mapping.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for wholemeal pasta as a food product; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single dry serving of wholemeal pasta is approximately 80 g (uncooked), which yields roughly 200–220 g when cooked and provides around 290 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Wholemeal pasta, uncooked — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Foodo record for wholemeal pasta, uncooked (cr_id F05070014). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and micronutrients not reported by HPB for this entry are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 20080 (Pasta, whole-wheat, dry) where available; fields not available in either source are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 49 (low) for whole wheat / wholemeal pasta (dry, cooked al dente) is documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Glycemic load per 80 g dry serving calculated as: (49 × 73.1 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 0.80) / 100 = 28.7 g. Input record cited GI 49 with confidence 0.3 from an embedded GI database; this has been upgraded to confidence 0.85 following cross-reference with the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (11 mg/100 g) and high potassium (366 mg/100 g) yield a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: Wholemeal pasta is an excellent base carbohydrate for hypertensive individuals; cook without added salt and pair with low-sodium vegetable-based sauces to preserve its inherent sodium advantage.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI of 49 combined with high dietary fibre (10.1 g/100 g) and moderate protein (13.5 g/100 g) significantly attenuates postprandial glucose response compared with refined pasta
type2_diabetes.guidance: Wholemeal pasta is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in controlled portions (approximately 60–80 g dry weight per meal) and cooked al dente to preserve the lower glycaemic response; monitor total carbohydrate intake as 73.1 g/100 g uncooked translates to approximately 27 g/100 g cooked.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High dietary fibre (10.1 g/100 g) supports LDL cholesterol reduction; total fat is low (2.68 g/100 g) with negligible saturated fat (~0.49 g/100 g) and zero trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Wholemeal pasta is a heart-healthy carbohydrate choice; its high fibre content contributes to LDL reduction and its low saturated fat profile is consistent with AHA dietary guidelines for cardiovascular risk reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High dietary fibre (10.1 g/100 g) and negligible saturated fat support an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Wholemeal pasta fits an anti-inflammatory diet; pair with olive oil, vegetables, or legume-based sauces and avoid high-saturated-fat meat sauces to maximise anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wholemeal pasta (uncooked) is a dried pasta product made from 100% whole wheat flour, retaining the bran and germ of the wheat grain. It has a characteristic brown colour, slightly coarser texture, and a nutty, wheaty flavour compared with refined white pasta. It is produced by extruding or rolling whole wheat dough into various shapes (spaghetti, penne, fusilli, etc.) and then drying. Per 100 g uncooked, it provides 362 kcal, 13.5 g protein, 2.68 g fat (of which only ~0.49 g is saturated), 73.1 g carbohydrate, 10.1 g dietary fibre, and just 11 mg sodium. Its glycemic index of 49 (low) makes it substantially more suitable for blood glucose management than refined pasta (GI ~65–70). The high fibre content supports gut health, LDL cholesterol reduction, and satiety. Potassium at 366 mg/100 g and the very low sodium content make it compatible with DASH dietary principles for hypertension management. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and inflammatory conditions when consumed in appropriate portions. A standard dry serving is 80 g, yielding approximately 200–220 g cooked. It is widely available in supermarkets globally and is a staple of Western and Mediterranean dietary patterns. It is vegan, vegetarian, halal, and dairy-free, but contains gluten and is not suitable for those with coeliac disease or wheat allergy.
common_user_queries: Is wholemeal pasta good for diabetics?
What is the GI of whole wheat pasta?
How many calories in wholemeal pasta?
Is wholemeal pasta high in fibre?
Wholemeal pasta vs white pasta nutrition
Is wholemeal pasta good for weight loss?
Can I eat wholemeal pasta with high blood pressure?
How much protein in wholemeal pasta?

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient data sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05070014, confidence 0.95). Fatty acid breakdown and selected micronutrients supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 20080 (Pasta, whole-wheat, dry), an approved secondary source. GI of 49 cross-referenced and confirmed against University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed international GI tables. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.49 g), monounsaturated fat (0.34 g), polyunsaturated fat (1.08 g), trans fat (0.0 g confirmed), cholesterol (0.0 g confirmed), and selected B-vitamins (thiamine, riboflavin, niacin, B6, folate), phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water content are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 20080 (Pasta, whole-wheat, dry) as these fields were not reported in the HPB Foodo record for this item. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae and vitamin_e_mg are set to -1: these values are not reported in either the HPB Foodo record or the USDA SR Legacy entry 20080 with sufficient specificity for wholemeal pasta; detailed values unavailable in both HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (entry 20080) as of 2026. 3. GI value of 49 from the input record's embedded GI database has been validated and upgraded to confidence 0.85 following cross-reference with the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Glycemic load per 80 g dry serving = (49 × 73.1 × 0.80) / 100 = 28.7. 4. No core macro (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) is zero; all are nutritionally substantive and consistent with whole wheat dried pasta. 5. This is a Western food item; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese multilingual naming requirements apply under G6. Local name fields are set to null accordingly. 6. FoodEx2 code A000F is used as a closest_parent mapping for dried whole wheat pasta; no single exact FoodEx2 code for wholemeal pasta uncooked was identified in the public FoodEx2 catalogue. The foodex2_match_type has been set to 'closest_parent' to accurately reflect this mapping relationship.

q1_food_identity.notes: canonical English name present and multilingual identity satisfied

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: core macros and nutrient source integrity pass

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: secondary nutrient fields are present and numeric

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: GI, GI category, GL, and GI source are complete

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: alias coverage is adequate and multilingual alias consistency passes

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from input, supplemented fatty acid and micronutrient data from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 20080, validated GI against University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008.
Iteration 2: Reviewed validator feedback; foodex2_match_type was 'adapted' which was flagged as invalid. Attempted correction but used same value.

Iteration 3: Corrected foodex2_match_type from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent', which is one of the three permitted values ['exact', 'closest_parent', 'none'] per the validator schema. Updated foodex2_gap_note to reflect closest_parent relationship. Updated notes field to document the correction. G5 hard gate now passes.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
